input_row.title: Ensure 'Versioning' is set to 'Enabled' on Azure Blob Storage storage accounts
input_row.description: Enabling blob versioning allows for the automatic retention of previous versions of objects. With blob versioning enabled, earlier versions of a blob are accessible for data recovery in the event of modifications or deletions.
input_row.rationale: Blob versioning safeguards data integrity and enables recovery by retaining previous versions of stored objects, facilitating quick restoration from accidental deletion, modification, or malicious activity. Impact: Enabling blob versioning for a storage account creates a new version with each write operation to a blob, which can increase storage costs. To control these costs, a lifecycle management policy can be applied to automatically delete older versions.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Storage accounts. 2. Click the name of a storage account with blob storage. 3. In the Overview page, on the Properties tab, under Blob service, ensure Versioning is set to Enabled. 4. Repeat steps 1-3 for each storage account with blob storage. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list storage accounts: az storage account list Run the following command to determine if a storage account has containers: az storage container list --account-name <storage-account> For each storage account with containers, ensure that the output of the below command contains "isVersioningEnabled": true: az storage account blob-service-properties show --account-name <storage- account>  Audit from PowerShell Run the following command to list storage accounts: Get-AzStorageAccount Run the following command to create an Azure Storage context for a storage account: $context = New-AzStorageContext -StorageAccountName <storage-account> Run the following command to list containers for the storage account: Get-AzStorageContainer -Context $context If the storage account has containers, run the following command to get the blob service properties of the storage account: $account = Get-AzStorageBlobServiceProperty -ResourceGroupName <resource- group> -AccountName <storage-account> Run the following command to get the blob versioning setting for the storage account: $account.IsVersioningEnabled Ensure that the command returns True. Repeat for each storage account. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: c36a325b-ae04-4863-ad4f-19c6678f8e08 - Name: 'Configure your Storage account to enable blob versioning'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Storage accounts. 2. Click the name of a storage account with blob storage. 3. In the Overview page, on the Properties tab, under Blob service, click Disabled next to Versioning. 4. Under Tracking, check the box next to Enable versioning for blobs. 5. Select the radio button next to Keep all versions or Delete versions after (in days). 6. If selecting to delete versions, enter a number of in the box after which to delete blob versions. 7. Click Save. 8. Repeat steps 1-7 for each storage account with blob storage. Remediate from Azure CLI For each storage account requiring remediation, run the following command to enable blob versioning: az storage account blob-service-properties update --account-name <storage- account> --enable-versioning true Remediate from PowerShell For each storage account requiring remediation, run the following command to enable blob versioning: Update-AzStorageBlobServiceProperty -ResourceGroupName <resource-group> - StorageAccountName <storage-account> -IsVersioningEnabled $true Default Value: Blob versioning is disabled by default on storage accounts. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/storage/account 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/storage/account/blob-service- properties 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/get- azstorageaccount 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/new- azstoragecontext 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/get- azstoragecontainer 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/get- azstorageblobserviceproperty 7. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/update- azstorageblobserviceproperty 8. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/versioning-overview 9. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/lifecycle-management- overview
input_row.impact: Enabling blob versioning for a storage account creates a new version with each write operation to a blob, which can increase storage costs. To control these costs, a lifecycle management policy can be applied to automatically delete older versions.
input_row.reference: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/storage/account 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/storage/account/blob-service- properties 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/get- azstorageaccount 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/new- azstoragecontext 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/get- azstoragecontainer 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/get- azstorageblobserviceproperty 7. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/update- azstorageblobserviceproperty 8. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/versioning-overview 9. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/lifecycle-management- overview

first_opinion.response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_storage_account_blob_versioning_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program will check the following:

- **Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to use:**
  - `az storage account list` to enumerate all storage accounts within the subscription.
  - `az storage container list --account-name <storage-account>` to check if each storage account contains any containers.
  - `az storage account blob-service-properties show --account-name <storage-account>` to determine the blob versioning status.

- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - All storage accounts across all subscriptions or specified resource groups.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - The property `isVersioningEnabled` should be checked to ensure it is set to `true`.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** If `isVersioningEnabled` is `true` for all blob storage accounts with containers.
  - **Fail:** If any blob storage account has `isVersioningEnabled` set to `false`.

- **Example API endpoint or CLI command:**
  - CLI command to check versioning status: 
    ```bash
    az storage account blob-service-properties show --account-name <storage-account>
    ```
  - In the output, the check would be:
    ```json
    "isVersioningEnabled": true
    ```

second_opinion.response:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively checked using Azure APIs and CLI commands, which are well-suited for automating the verification of configuration settings across multiple resources.
- The use of Azure CLI commands to enumerate storage accounts and check the blob versioning status is appropriate and aligns with Azure's capabilities for automation.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly accurate. The use of `az storage account blob-service-properties show` is correct for checking the versioning status.
- However, the step involving `az storage container list` is unnecessary for this specific control, as versioning is a property of the storage account's blob service, not individual containers.
- The program name `azure_storage_account_blob_versioning_enabled` is clear and follows a logical naming convention, though it could be slightly more descriptive by including "check" or similar to indicate its purpose.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- Remove the step involving `az storage container list`, as it does not contribute to the assessment of versioning status.
- Consider including a check for permissions to ensure the script has the necessary access to query blob service properties.
- Provide a fallback or error-handling mechanism in the script to manage API rate limits or failures gracefully.
- Include a note on ensuring the Azure CLI is authenticated and configured to access the correct subscription context.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is technically sound with minor improvements suggested. The use of Azure CLI is appropriate for this control, and the overall approach is well-structured.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- Modify the assessment to remove the unnecessary step of listing containers, as it does not impact the evaluation of blob versioning.
- Enhance the automation script with error handling and permission checks to improve robustness and reliability.

final_decision.response:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the audit can be automated using Azure CLI commands, which are capable of verifying the blob versioning status across storage accounts. The necessary properties are accessible through Azure's APIs, specifically using the `az storage account blob-service-properties show` command to check if versioning is enabled. This aligns with Azure's capabilities for automation and ensures efficient compliance checks across multiple resources.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
azure_storage_account_blob_versioning_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Azure Resource/Service: Storage Accounts
- API/CLI Method: `az storage account blob-service-properties show`
- Properties to Check: `isVersioningEnabled`
- Pass Criteria: `isVersioningEnabled` is `true` for all storage accounts with blob storage.
- Fail Criteria: `isVersioningEnabled` is `false` or not set for any storage account with blob storage.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions. The second opinion provided a minor critique regarding the unnecessary use of `az storage container list`, which was acknowledged and excluded from the final automation details.
